mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health, triggering profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered months or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced capacity utilization, causing short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 200 while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Triggered periods show positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases over the evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue expansion and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth signals yield negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt, leading to a mean-reversion sell-off in equities over the following month.
failure_condition: Divergence signals produce positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of cases during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust capacity expansion, triggering short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery yield negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances.
